mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a robust economic expansion, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Employment is a key coincident indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: The 6-month change in PAYEMS does not have a statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds a high threshold, it signals severe economic distress and depressed consumer demand, leading to lower corporate profits and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:At decision time, UNRATE is 9.7%, well above the trigger threshold, reflecting the post-GFC environment.
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return is not statistically significantly negative during periods when UNRATE is above 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating inflation and low unemployment signals an overheating economy, increasing the likelihood of monetary policy tightening, which raises discount rates and negatively impacts equity valuations.
failure_condition: An interaction term between CPIAUCSL momentum and UNRATE level is not a statistically significant negative predictor of 60-day returns in a walk-forward regression model.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low real GDP growth, strong retail sales growth is a powerful signal of improving consumer demand, leading to upward revisions in earnings forecasts and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between RSAFS momentum and forward returns is not significantly higher when YoY GDPC1 growth is below 2% compared to when it is above 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A negative change in industrial production indicates a contraction in the manufacturing and production sectors, leading to lower earnings and negative pressure on equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a core component of the Conference Board Leading Economic Index.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in INDPRO has a rolling 36-month correlation with 20-day forward returns that is not consistently negative (i.e., correlation > -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed Funds rate is near the zero lower bound, negative shocks to real activity have an amplified negative effect on equities because conventional monetary policy has limited room to respond.
evidence_refs: fact:ZIRP policy was in effect post-2008 crisis.
failure_condition: The negative beta of S&P 500 returns to negative INDPRO shocks is not statistically larger when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% compared to when it is above 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and investment, which are leading indicators of a broader economic slowdown, negatively impacting future equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:The housing market collapse was central to the 2008 financial crisis.
failure_condition: Instances of HOUST falling more than 10% MoM are not followed by negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, unexpected monthly increase in the consumer price index signals rising input costs for corporations and increases the probability of future monetary tightening, both negative for short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following CPI releases that show a MoM increase greater than 0.5% is not statistically different from zero.
